proj-asympt.coreProblem: Asymmetric parallel transformer for multi-organ medical CT segmentation on the Synapse 9-class dataset, targeting high Dice scores at moderate parameter count and FLOPs.
proj-asympt.architectureDetails: A ~35-40M parameter transformer-based segmentation model with asymmetric parallel branches in the encoder, mixing global attention and local convolutions. Uses CNNs at high resolutions, a Parallel Hybrid Block with Gated Fusion at mid-scale, and Dual-Swin blocks at the bottleneck for global context modelling. Training uses a composite CombinedLoss mixing Dice loss, cross-entropy, and Focal Loss for hard pixels on small organs, plus label smoothing and EMA weight averaging. Augmentation pipeline includes random elastic deformation, multi-scale zoom+crop (0.8-1.2x), cutout/random erasing, flips, rotations, brightness/contrast, Gaussian noise/blur. Evaluation uses visualize_predictions_grid with per-slice Dice and error maps classifying correct foreground, false negatives, false positives, and misclassifications.
suggestedBullets: Co-designed AsymPT, an asymmetric parallel transformer for Synapse multi-organ CT segmentation (~35-40M parameters, ~28B FLOPs) achieving 84% Dice Score at 28B FLOPs vs TransUNet's 77.48% at 106B FLOPs, delivering a superior efficiency-performance tradeoff.

Incorporated a composite loss function with Focal Loss and label smoothing plus an EMA weight-averaging wrapper to stabilize training, reduce overconfidence, and improve small-organ Dice scores for challenging classes like gallbladder and esophagus.

Deployed a production-ready training pipeline with mixed-precision AMP, deep supervision (3-level weighted loss), cosine annealing LR, and multi-format inference support (NIfTI, PNG, NPY), with an augmentation toolkit including elastic deformations, random scale+crop, and cutout.

proj-dasai.coreProblem: Multi-tenant Discord copilot that combines a production Discord bot with a Supabase-backed RAG system, per-guild configuration, and an authenticated admin console for ops/research on top of pgvector.
proj-dasai.architectureDetails: Discord.py 2.x bot with HuggingFace InferenceClient for chat (Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct) and embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2). RAG pipeline: knowledge_documents table with embedding vector(384) and IVFFlat cosine index (lists=100). Search RPC search_documents uses 1-(embedding<=>query_embedding) with threshold and limit, followed by local NumPy cosine re-ranking and regex sentence-split snippet extraction. Web search decision via classifier sub-prompt or keyword triggers, then DuckDuckGo augmentation. Conversation memory: per-channel rolling summaries updated every 5 messages via LLM. Multi-tenant: bot_config per guild, user_roles with team_lead/member RBAC, guild_config_cache with 60s TTL. Admin console: Next.js App Router with Supabase SSR/CSR clients, role-based access, live-edit of prompts/channels/roles/RAG docs. Deployed as Dockerized Railway service.
suggestedBullets: Designed and deployed a multi-tenant Discord copilot that stores per-guild configuration, roles, message history, and pgvector-backed RAG documents in Supabase using vector(384) embeddings and IVFFlat cosine indexes for fast similarity search.

Programmed an HF InferenceClient integration that drives both chat and embedding calls via asyncio executors, runs a classifier sub-prompt to decide when to web-search, and injects DuckDuckGo summaries plus RAG snippets into the final system prompt while enforcing channel allowlists and team-lead/member roles.

Developed an authenticated Next.js admin console using Supabase SSR/CSR clients to manage guild-level prompts, allowed channels, conversation summaries, user roles, and RAG documents, wired into a production Docker + Railway deployment.

proj-irpai.coreProblem: Image Resolution Pipeline AI that turns raw UI screenshots into structured ContextPacks (JSON/Markdown/system prompts) for LLM agents — a perception layer for screen-understanding and agentic UI automation.
proj-irpai.architectureDetails: 5-stage pipeline: Stage 1 — RT-DETR (real-time DETR variant) fine-tuned on custom mobile/mixed UI dataset via HuggingFace Transformers outputs bounding boxes + labels for UI elements. Rule-based region detector as non-ML fallback. Default checkpoint: local checkpoints/rtdetr-ui-mobile-mixed-complete/checkpoint-best, with fallback to PekingU/rtdetr_r18vd_coco_o365. Stage 2 — EasyOCR extracts multilingual text with confidence scores per detected region. Stage 3 — ContextPack building: aggregates detection + OCR into unified data structure with IDs, bounding boxes, text content, inferred roles, hierarchical layout. Output formats: JSON object, Markdown, or system prompt string for LLM injection.
suggestedBullets: Engineered a 5-stage machine vision pipeline converting raw screenshots into structured JSON/Markdown at ~287ms end-to-end latency for downstream LLM consumption, combining RT-DETR object detection with EasyOCR text extraction.
Fine-tuned RT-DETR on custom UI datasets with a hybrid detector-fallback strategy, extracting bounding boxes, semantic roles, reading order, and confidence scores for desktop/mobile UI elements.
Packaged as a pip-installable Python library with CLI, modular config system, pytest suite, and dedicated fine-tuning scripts for custom dataset adaptation.

proj-slm.coreProblem: Financial Small Language Model trained from scratch in PyTorch via a two-phase curriculum (general English → financial corpora) and served through a Streamlit app (FinPeak.ai) for interactive financial Q&A.
proj-slm.architectureDetails: GPT-style architecture with GPTConfig dataclass (block_size=256, vocab_size=50257, n_layer=8, n_head=12, n_embd=768, dropout=0.14). Core blocks: custom LayerNorm, CausalSelfAttention with F.scaled_dot_product_attention (flash attention aware), MLP with GELU and 4x expansion, residual Block composition. Weight tying between wte and lm_head. Two-phase curriculum: Phase 1 trains on general English corpora (OpenWebText/Alpaca) with AdamW (betas=0.9,0.95, weight_decay=0.1), warmup LinearLR + CosineAnnealingLR, GradScaler for AMP. Phase 2 fine-tunes on financial datasets (Finance Alpaca, Financial PhraseBank) with lower LR, best_val_loss tracking. All data unified under instruction template format and tokenized with tiktoken GPT-2 encoding. Served via Streamlit app that downloads safetensors from HuggingFace satgun/finpeak-ai.
suggestedBullets: Architected a GPT-style transformer from scratch in PyTorch with tied embeddings, flash-attention-aware causal self-attention, and GELU-based MLP blocks, trained via a two-phase curriculum (general English → financial corpora) with AdamW, warmup+cosine LR, gradient accumulation, and mixed-precision AMP.

Applied LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning on FinancialPhraseBank and FiQA, reducing trainable parameters from millions to thousands while preserving domain expressiveness for sentiment classification and buy/sell/hold signal generation.

Deployed the model as FinPeak.ai, a Streamlit chat app that downloads safetensors weights from HuggingFace, reconstructs GPTConfig, and generates instruction-formatted financial answers with controllable temperature and response length.

proj-sih.coreProblem: Cloud and network infrastructure for Smart India Hackathon using AWS EC2, Docker, PostgreSQL, n8n workflow automation, and AWS S3.
proj-sih.architectureDetails: Dockerized n8n workflow automation and PostgreSQL services on AWS EC2 with persistent volumes and secure inter-container networking. AWS S3 bucket integration for scalable cloud storage. End-to-end infrastructure setup delivered under 36-hour hackathon constraints.
suggestedBullets: Deployed Dockerized n8n workflow automation and PostgreSQL services on AWS EC2 with persistent volumes and secure inter-container networking.
Integrated an AWS S3 bucket for scalable cloud storage and delivered end-to-end infrastructure setup under 36-hour hackathon constraints.

proj-cloudtunnel.coreProblem: Persistent cloud tunnel and automated ML workflow engine using Raspberry Pi as an always-on edge node with Cloudflare networking and Docker-based n8n automation.
proj-cloudtunnel.architectureDetails: Raspberry Pi configured as always-on edge node with Cloudflare Warp and Tunnel for persistent secure connectivity bypassing network outages and captive portals. Docker Compose with persistent volumes and automated restart policies to keep n8n running continuously with data preserved across reboots.
suggestedBullets: Configured a Raspberry Pi as an always-on edge node with Cloudflare Warp and Tunnel to establish persistent secure connectivity bypassing network outages and captive portals.
Configured Docker Compose with persistent volumes and automated restart policies to keep n8n running continuously with data preserved across reboots.

proj-iqagent.coreProblem: Local-first CLI tool that turns a candidate's PDF resume into tailored technical interview questions and model answers using a locally hosted Ollama LLM, ensuring 100% data privacy.
proj-iqagent.architectureDetails: pypdf.PdfReader for resume text extraction. LangChain PromptTemplate with structured Instruction/Input/Response format. ChatOllama targeting gemma3:4b model at http://127.0.0.1:11434 with temperature=0.7. Chain: prompt | llm. Advanced variant: interactive multi-line job description capture from stdin with sentinel termination. Output persisted to interview_questions.txt as markdown-friendly text.
suggestedBullets: Developed a localized Python CLI tool that parses PDF resumes with pypdf and generates structured technical interview questions with sample answers using Ollama (Gemma-3-4b), ensuring 100% data privacy via fully offline inference.
Integrated target Job Description matching to align generated questions with specific role requirements, with an interactive advanced CLI that captures multi-line JDs from stdin.
Structured output as markdown-friendly text persisted to interview_questions.txt, enabling reuse in interview prep workflows and easy versioning under source control.

proj-outreach-agent.coreProblem: AI-driven resume customization system using Spring Boot and LangChain4j to automatically generate tailored, ATS-compliant, single-page PDF resumes from a master knowledge base.
proj-outreach-agent.architectureDetails: Spring Boot REST API (POST /api/resume/generate). LangChain4j AiService with 3 tools: ResumeKnowledgeBaseTool (tag-filtered master resume search), DocumentGeneratorTool (Thymeleaf + Jsoup + Flying Saucer PDF generation), PageLengthCheckerTool (OpenPDF page count validation). MasterResumeService: loads master_resume.json at startup, filterByTags scores and sorts by match count with non-empty fallbacks. PdfGeneratorService: normalizes bullet structures, builds XHTML via Thymeleaf, parses with Jsoup XML parser, compiles via ITextRenderer. Agent system prompt enforces 7-step deterministic workflow with iterative page-length feedback loop (up to 3 retries). Dual LLM backend: GoogleAiGeminiChatModel (gemini-2.5-flash) or OpenAiChatModel via OpenRouter. MessageWindowChatMemory with max 20 messages. SMTP integration via JavaMailSender for automated resume delivery.
suggestedBullets: Developed an agentic Spring Boot system using LangChain4j with a 3-tool architecture (knowledge base search, PDF generation, page-length validation) to automatically customize resumes targeting job descriptions.

Engineered a robust batch processing pipeline with an AtomicBoolean concurrency lock to prevent race conditions across parallel LLM invocations, alongside duplicate target prevention and exponential backoff for web scraping.
Engineered a persistent PDF caching system using SHA-256 hashing on job descriptions and scraped company research to bypass redundant LLM generation, saving API costs and reducing latency.
Designed an automated skill sorting algorithm to dynamically align resume keywords with parsed job requirements, eliminating LLM hallucination and enforcing strict ATS compliance.

proj-video-summary.coreProblem: Resumable multimodal meeting/lecture summarizer CLI that fuses Whisper audio transcription, OpenCV key-frame extraction, and Ollama vision+text LLMs to produce structured markdown summaries.
proj-video-summary.architectureDetails: Pipeline: Stage 1 — ffmpeg extracts mono 16kHz WAV audio (idempotent check). Stage 2 — Whisper transcription with configurable model size (tiny-large), checkpointed to checkpoint_transcript.json. Stage 3 — OpenCV frame extraction at configurable intervals (cv2.VideoCapture, frame_interval = fps * interval_sec). Stage 4a — LLava vision model describes each frame via base64-encoded JPEG + ollama.chat. Stage 4b — LLaMA 3.2 generates structured summary from transcript (first 6000 chars) + visual context (first 3000 chars) with sections: Overview, Key Topics, Screen Content Highlights, Action Items. Stage 5 — CLI with per-stage --force flags to selectively invalidate checkpoints. Output: summary.md + frame_descriptions.json. setup.sh: validates Python/ffmpeg/Ollama, creates venv, installs deps, pulls llava and llama3 models.
suggestedBullets: Engineered a resumable multimodal summarization CLI that extracts audio via ffmpeg, transcribes with OpenAI Whisper, samples key frames using OpenCV, and calls Ollama vision/text models (LLaVA + LLaMA 3.2) to produce structured markdown summaries.
Configured per-stage checkpointing (transcript, frames, frame descriptions, summary) with --force overrides, enabling large-file resilience and efficient iterative runs on long meetings.
Packaged with a portable setup.sh that validates Python/ffmpeg/Ollama prerequisites, creates a venv, and pulls required Ollama models for one-command bootstrap.

proj-django-news.coreProblem: Django 5 + Django REST Framework news aggregation service with API-key-protected endpoints for retrieving, searching, and ranking news articles using custom scoring functions.
proj-django-news.architectureDetails: Django 5 with DRF. Models: News (title, content, updated, date, views), Comment (user FK, news FK, body, timestamps), apiKey (user FK, api_key), Log (log_info). Serializers: NewsSerializer and CommentSerializer with fields='__all__'. URL routing: path-parameterized endpoints like /list/<id>/apikey=<key> and /api/news/mode=<mode>/apikey=<key>. Manual API-key validation at each endpoint. Mode-based endpoints: mode=1 getlatest (max updated timestamp), mode=2 getpopular (max views), mode=3 getbest (custom scorer: 0.3 × time_of_day + 0.7 × views). Search: substring matching on News.content. HTML views for login/register/homepage. Per-article view counter incremented on each access. Log table for auditability.
suggestedBullets: Deployed a Django 5 + DRF API with path-parameterized endpoints and manual API-key validation, supporting list, search, random, and mode-based news retrieval with a custom 'best' scorer combining recency (0.3 weight) and popularity (0.7 weight).
Designed a relational schema with News, Comment, apiKey, and Log models, using ModelSerializers for JSON responses while serving traditional HTML templates for authentication and browsing.
Encoded 'latest', 'popular', and 'best' selection logic as dedicated aggregation functions over SQLite, with per-article view counters and human-readable access logs in a Log table for observability.

proj-reliance-001.coreProblem: Unsupervised anomaly detection systems for industrial flowmeters and control valves in a secure Accops VDI environment, processing refinery telemetry to identify stiction and pipeline abnormalities.
proj-reliance-001.architectureDetails: Deep Autoencoder models with SELU activation, AlphaDropout, and reconstruction loss (MAE). Domain-driven feature engineering: RMS-normalized deltas, sliding-window cosine similarity, normalized flow deltas, and valve transition states. Bayesian hyperparameter optimization from scratch via Gaussian Processes (RBF kernel) and Expected Improvement. Data pipeline: Pandas, Plotly, Keras, Matplotlib, TensorFlow.
suggestedBullets: Engineered RMS-normalized flow deltas and cosine similarity features on sliding windows across all pairwise sensor combinations, improving anomaly detection recall by 14% and reducing processing latency by 25%.

Developed deep Autoencoder models (SELU, AlphaDropout, MAE loss) for unsupervised anomaly detection across 5 industrial systems, achieving up to 99.47% training accuracy and 93.05% validation accuracy on large multi-sensor time-series data.

Applied Bayesian hyperparameter optimization from scratch using Gaussian Processes (RBF kernel) and Expected Improvement to auto-tune training parameters, accelerating search efficiency by 3.5x over standard grid search.
Delivered dashboards, technical documentation, and presentations to mentors, leads, and stakeholders, honing cross-functional communication in a production ML environment.

proj-iqilo.coreProblem: Next.js App Router learning OS that models structured day-by-day roadmaps for DSA, competitive programming, and quantitative finance, wrapped in a gamification engine (XP, levels, streaks, badges) and a persistent progress store that auto-resumes users exactly where they left off.
proj-iqilo.architectureDetails: Next.js (App Router) with TypeScript; client components using 'use client'. TailwindCSS v4 with CSS variables design system (var(--bg-surface), var(--text-primary)). Course model: lib/courses.ts pulls strongly-typed Course objects from multiple @/data/courses/... modules for DSA, competitive programming, and Python-for-quants tracks. Each Course has weeks and days; getDayByNumber() traverses weeks to locate Day objects. Progress store: hooks/useProgress.tsx defines ProgressProvider around an external ProgressStore using useSyncExternalStore for both progress and XP/streak GamificationSnapshot. loadProgress() hydrates from localStorage; saveProgress() persists; commitStore() notifies subscribers via a Set of listeners. Hydration signaled by a separate useSyncExternalStore flag. Gamification: independent xpStore with getXpSnapshot() reading XP, deriving level via getLevelFromXp, computing streaks via getCurrentStreak/getLongestStreak, and loading badges. Cross-tab sync via storage events on 'iqilo-xp' key and custom 'iqilo-xp-update' events. checkAchievements() calls checkAchievementsFn(store, listCourses()). HomePage orchestration: computes lastCourse from store[c.id]?.lastVisitedDay, auto-redirects via sessionStorage key 'iqilo_auto_redirected' once per session to /courses/{lastCourse.course.id}/day/{lastDay}. Skeleton UI with animate-pulse while hydrated===false. Components: RecommendedQuests (top 3 courses), CertificateCard, LearningVelocityGraph (getActivityMap), HelpSection.
suggestedBullets: Architected a Next.js/TypeScript learning OS where immutable course roadmaps and day-level units across DSA, competitive programming, and quantitative finance tracks are wired into an external ProgressStore via useSyncExternalStore, supporting instant 'continue where you left off' session redirects based on lastVisitedDay per course.

Formulated a gamification subsystem that derives XP levels, daily/longest streaks, and unlocked badges from localStorage-backed stores, synchronizing changes across tabs via browser storage events and a custom 'iqilo-xp-update' event while keeping UI reactive through external-store subscriptions.

Developed a fully client-side progress API (useProgress) exposing day/resource toggles, per-week/per-course completion stats, JSON export, and auto-redirect logic, alongside a TailwindCSS v4 dashboard with recommended quests, certificate cards, and a learning velocity graph.

proj-nvidia-claude-bridge.coreProblem: Custom litellm callback layer that safely bridges Anthropic Claude Code (and similar IDE-integrated LLM clients) to NVIDIA NIM models, enforcing per-model rate limits, stripping mismatched multimodal payloads from text-only backends, and surfacing rich diagnostic logging for failures including HTTP 429 rate-limit responses.
proj-nvidia-claude-bridge.architectureDetails: Structured around litellm.integrations.custom_logger.CustomLogger extended via CustomRateLimitLogger implementing pre-call hooks and failure logging. AsyncRateLimiter(rpm=40) maintains a lock-protected deque of timestamps; acquire(model_name) drops entries older than 60s, computes sleep_time = history[0] + 60.1 - now when at limit, logs yellow [RateLimiter] warning and awaits asyncio.sleep. ModelRateLimiter holds dict of AsyncRateLimiter keyed by model name with global_lock for lazy init. async_pre_call_hook: clamps max_tokens to 4096; for non-vision models strips image_url/image typed blocks from message content lists, injects dummy text block when all blocks removed; logs red [WARNING] about Claude Code image attempt; calls rate_limiter.acquire(model_name). is_vision_model() checks for 'vision', '-vl', 'paligemma', 'multimodal' in lowercase name. Failure logging: _log_failure extracts exception, derives status_code from exception.status_code or exception.response.status_code. Rate-limit detection via status_code==429 or 'rate' in exception type/message. Dumps all non-private exception attributes, response headers (flags retry-after and limit keys), response body (raw text + parsed JSON). Inspects response_obj attributes. Global proxy_handler_instance = CustomRateLimitLogger() exported for litellm attachment.
suggestedBullets: Implemented a per-model asynchronous rate limiter (ModelRateLimiter + AsyncRateLimiter) around litellm that enforces a 40 RPM cap in-memory using a lock-protected timestamp deque, preventing downstream NVIDIA NIM 429 responses and emitting human-readable warnings when limits are reached.

Established a multimodal safety layer in async_pre_call_hook that clamps max_tokens and strips all image payloads from messages for text-only models, injecting a dummy text block when needed and logging explicit warnings when Claude Code attempts to send images to non-vision backends.

Engineered a rich failure introspection system that detects rate-limit conditions (HTTP 429 or 'rate' signatures), dumps all exception attributes, NIM response headers (including retry-after/limit fields), raw and parsed JSON bodies, plus response_obj attributes to enable rapid debugging of bridge failures.
